goal: Mengganti palet Aurora Terminal menjadi tema gelap netral profesional dan memperbarui design tokens global.
summary: Phase 1 completed: theme tokens in `tailwind.config.ts`, `app/globals.css`, and `DESIGN.md` have been migrated from Aurora cyan/magenta to a neutral professional dark palette. Signal green/red are reserved for BUY/SELL. Typecheck and lint pass.
stepSummaries:
1. Update color/shadow tokens in `tailwind.config.ts` to neutral dark palette; keep `buy`/`sell` as signal green/red, add/rename accent/info/warn tokens; remove or rename `aurora` shadow. [verified]
  2. Update CSS variables, `.btn-primary`, `.btn-danger`, badges, glow, shimmer, focus/selection, and body gradient in `app/globals.css` to neutral palette. [verified]
  3. Update `DESIGN.md` color spec and directional-color rule to reflect neutral theme. [verified]
  4. Run typecheck and lint to ensure token changes compile. [verified]

reviewerRationale: 2 block flag(s) raised — see attached gate verdicts and project checks.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Steps 1-3 used targeted grep (proxy) to confirm removal of old colors, which is appropriate for static config edits. Step 4 ran real `tsc --noEmit` and ESLint (syntactic), covering the changed artifacts. No step relied solely on proxy/sentinel.
gateVerdicts.evidence: tailwind.config.ts grep found only rename comment; globals.css grep returned 0 matches; DESIGN.md grep returned 0 matches; `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` exited 0.

gateVerdicts.rationale: The phase goal was to replace the Aurora palette with a neutral terminal dark theme; the three files that define the design system now use the new palette and pass typecheck/lint.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Updated `tailwind.config.ts` colors, `app/globals.css` primitives, and `DESIGN.md` spec; verification commands pass.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Nothing deferred in this phase. Non-signal usages of `buy`/`sell` in components will be addressed in Phase 2 as planned.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Phase 2 step list explicitly covers decoupling non-signal color usage across components.
